summary: Matched two small previously-placeholder functions: grMaterial_801C95C4 in stage material code and it_802B5974 in Samus chargeshot item code. The PR also extended itSamusChargeshot_ItemVars with padding and an x28 field at item-var offset +DFC so the chargeshot destroy/reset helper could write the correct per-item variable. Evidence is limited to the diff; there were no review comments and no PR body excerpt in the provided slice.
what_changed: Implemented grMaterial_801C95C4(HSD_GObj* gobj), retrieving Ground* from gobj->user_data, calling lb_80014498 on a ColorOverlay located at byte offset 0x40 into the Ground object, then setting gp->x10_flags.b4 = 1.
Added include dependency lb/lb_00F9.h to grmaterial.c for lb_80014498.
Extended itSamusChargeshot_ItemVars in itCharItems.h with explicit padding from +DE0 to +DFC and a new s32 x28 field at +DFC.

Implemented it_802B5974(Item_GObj* gobj), guarded against NULL gobj, fetched Item* via GET_ITEM, destroyed all effects with efLib_DestroyAll, zeroed ip->xDD4_itemVar.samuschargeshot.x28, then called Item_8026A8EC(gobj).
Added include dependencies ef/eflib.h and it/item.h to itsamuschargeshot.c.
smart_moves: Used a narrow NULL guard in it_802B5974 before touching item data or destroying effects, matching the likely control-flow shape for a simple cleanup routine.
Modeled the Samus chargeshot item variable with explicit padding and a named field at the exact accessed offset instead of using an unexplained raw offset in the function body.
Kept the stage material match small and direct: user_data to Ground*, offset cast to ColorOverlay*, library helper call, then flag update.
Added only the headers needed by the newly matched functions, keeping dependency changes minimal.
decomp_lessons: For item-var unions/overlays, a function accessing a far offset can justify extending the per-item struct with explicit padding plus a provisional field name, preserving layout while enabling typed access.

When a matched function calls a known library routine on a subobject embedded inside a larger struct, a byte-offset cast such as (ColorOverlay*) ((u8*) gp + 0x40) can be an acceptable intermediate representation until the containing struct is better documented.

Cleanup/destructor-like item routines often combine effect cleanup, per-item state reset, and a common item helper call; matching these can unlock simple functions once the correct item-var field is declared.
Small placeholder replacements can still require header work: matching often depends on exposing helper prototypes and concrete struct accessors rather than changing logic alone.
naming_conventions: Kept unresolved functions under address-style names such as grMaterial_801C95C4 and it_802B5974.
Used project-standard item object aliases Item_GObj and Item with GET_ITEM(gobj) for item data access.
Used provisional struct member names x0, x4, x8, x28 in itSamusChargeshot_ItemVars where semantic names were not established.
Named explicit padding pad_8 even though the added padding begins after the existing x8 member; this reflects the local struct naming style in the diff rather than a reviewed semantic name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced an explicit char padding array sized as 0xDFC - 0xDE0 to force the next Samus chargeshot item-var field to land at offset +DFC.
Used a byte-pointer cast through u8* to access an embedded ColorOverlay at Ground-relative offset 0x40.
Ordered it_802B5974 operations as GET_ITEM, efLib_DestroyAll, zero item-var field, Item_8026A8EC, which is likely important for matching instruction order.
Used a local Item* variable rather than repeatedly expanding GET_ITEM, likely matching the original register/dataflow shape.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or document itSamusChargeshot_ItemVars.x28.
Find later PRs that replace the raw Ground + 0x40 ColorOverlay cast with a named Ground struct field.
Search for other item cleanup functions using efLib_DestroyAll followed by Item_8026A8EC.
Check whether grMaterial_801C9604 or adjacent grMaterial functions were matched after this PR.
